paper.title: The Chemical Composition of Extreme-Velocity Stars

extraction.summary: The paper studies 15 late-type extreme-velocity star candidates and explicitly concludes they are bound to the Galactic potential, with total velocities 274-520 km/s that do not exceed the Galactic escape velocity. No objects are treated as possibly unbound from the Milky Way.

method_chain.summary: Adopted Gaia EDR3 astrometry and photometry, combined with candidate lists from Hattori et al. (2018a) and Herzog et al. (2018) based on Gaia DR1.

source_refs.context: Sample selection from Hattori 2018a and Herzog 2018 with Gaia DR1 data

method_chain.summary: Selected 15 stars with quoted total velocities >~300 km/s and no previous high-resolution spectroscopic analysis, prioritizing brighter targets.

source_refs.context: Sample selection criteria and target prioritization

method_chain.summary: Measured spectroscopic radial velocities using ARCES at the 3.5m APO telescope and MIKE at the 6.5m Magellan telescope, with iSpec for RV calculation and barycentric corrections.

source_refs.context: Observation instruments and iSpec radial velocity processing

method_chain.summary: Derived distances by fitting MIST isochrones to photometric and astrometric data using the isochrones package with MultiNest, restricted by Bailer-Jones 2021 photo-geometric distances.

source_refs.context: Isochrone fitting with isochrones package and MultiNest for distance estimation

method_chain.summary: Calculated total Galactic velocities from Gaia EDR3 astrometry, spectroscopic radial velocities, and isochrone-derived distances.

source_refs.context: Total velocity calculation using Gaia EDR3 astrometry and spectroscopic RVs

method_chain.summary: Adopted the MWPotential2014 Galactic potential in galpy for orbit integration, consisting of a power-law bulge, Miyamoto-Nagai disk, and NFW halo.

source_refs.context: Adopted MWPotential2014 Galactic potential model description

method_chain.summary: Integrated Galactic orbits using galpy with the MWPotential2014 potential to derive pericenter, apocenter, eccentricity, zmax, total energy, and angular momentum.

Assessed bound status by comparing total velocities to the Galactic escape velocity. The paper concludes all 15 stars are bound to the Galactic potential with velocities 274-520 km/s, below the escape threshold.

source_refs.context: Abstract stating stars are not fast enough to be classified as hypervelocity stars
Conclusion stating stars are bound to the Galactic potential

method_chain.summary: Evaluated astrophysical origin using chemical abundances and orbital parameters, concluding the stars are likely tidal debris from disrupted dwarf galaxies accelerated by the Galactic potential.

source_refs.context: Conclusion on tidal debris origin from disrupted dwarf galaxies

candidate_groups_considered.description: 15 late-type extreme-velocity star candidates with photometric properties, stellar atmospheric parameters, and orbital parameters (RV, total velocity, pericenter, apocenter, eccentricity, zmax, total energy, angular momentum).
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly concludes that all 15 stars are bound to the Galactic potential, with total velocities of 274-520 km/s that do not exceed the Galactic escape velocity. The paper classifies them as extreme-velocity stars, not as hypervelocity stars or Galactic-unbound candidates.

source_refs.context: Abstract: total velocities 274-520 km/s; stars are not fast enough to be classified as hypervelocity stars and are extreme-velocity stars
Introduction: term HVSs used to describe extreme-velocity stars but not necessarily unbound
Conclusion: total velocities confirm they are bound to the Galactic potential and not as fast as expected for classical hypervelocity stars

candidate_groups_considered.description: Observation log for the 15 target stars, recording Gaia EDR3 source identifiers, coordinates, observation details, and instruments.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: This is an observation log, not a candidate table. The objects listed here are the same 15 extreme-velocity stars whose bound status is explicitly confirmed by the paper.

source_refs.context: Table caption and observation log for the 15 target stars
